title: NOC 14300 – Library Assistants and Clerks | GTR Immigration
description: Library Assistants and Clerks under NOC 14300 play an essential administrative and public service role within libraries and information centres across Canada. As educational institutions, public libraries, and community learning centres continue to expand digital and physical services, demand for support staff in this TEER 4 occupation remains stable nationwide. Qualified candidates may be eligible under certain employer-driven immigration streams and selected Provincial Nominee Programs.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category1 – Business, finance, and administration occupationsTEER4 – Occupations usually require a secondary school diploma or several weeks of on-the-job trainingMajor Group14 – Administrative and financial support and supply chain logistics occupationsSub-Major Group143 – Library, correspondence, and other clerksMinor Group1430 – Library, correspondence, and other clerksNOC Code14300

duties: Library Assistants and Clerks perform some or all of the following:
Issue and receive books and other materials
Sort, shelve, and reshelve library materials
Assist patrons in locating materials and accessing digital resources
Perform clerical filing and record-keeping tasks
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required
Basic computer and customer service skills are often required
While this occupation is generally non-regulated, employers may require prior clerical or administrative experience.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $27,000 to $57,800, depending on province, institution type, and experience level.
job_outlook: Digital literacy and database management skills may improve employment competitiveness.
labour_market_demand: Library Assistants and Clerks are employed in:
Employment opportunities are available across major provinces including Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Quebec, Manitoba, and Saskatchewan.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 14300?
Professionals in this occupation benefit from:
Opportunities in educational and government sectors
Potential transition into higher administrative or library technician roles
Pathways under certain employer-sponsored or provincial programs
This occupation provides a structured entry point into Canada’s administrative and education sector workforce.
pathways: If you are qualified under NOC 14300, you may be eligible to immigrate to Canada through:
Selected community or regional immigration pilots
